site.name: JoinMyPDF
site.defaultTitle: JoinMyPDF — Private PDF tools in your browser
site.defaultDescription: Merge, split, compress, and convert PDFs locally in your browser. No watermark on standard output, no forced signup for typical use.

categories.label: Convert
categories.description: Switch between image and PDF formats with browser-side processing.

categories.label: Optimize
categories.description: Reduce PDF delivery size while preserving practical readability.

categories.label: Edit
categories.description: Merge and split document flows for team-ready output.

categories.label: Security
categories.description: Password protection and privacy controls processed locally in your browser.

tools.title: Merge PDF Online
tools.intent: Merge multiple PDF files quickly in one browser session.
tools.description: Merge PDF files online with drag-and-drop ordering and instant in-browser output.
useCases: Combine monthly reports into one management packet
Merge legal forms and attachments before submission
Create onboarding document bundles for HR teams

faq.q: Can I merge more than two PDF files at once?
faq.a: Yes. Add multiple PDFs and drag them into the exact order you need.

faq.q: Is PDF merge processing private?
faq.a: Yes. Files are processed client-side in your browser.

faq.q: Will page quality change after merging?
faq.a: The merge workflow copies pages into one file without intentional quality loss.

faq.q: Can I merge PDFs on mobile?
faq.a: Yes. The upload and reorder UI is responsive for mobile browsers.

longTailPages.angle: Speed-first merging for quick delivery workflows
Touch-friendly PDF merge workflow for phones

tools.title: Compress PDF Online
tools.intent: Reduce PDF file size for sharing and faster downloads.
tools.description: Compress PDF files online using browser-based optimization controls and instant download.
useCases: Shrink proposal decks for email attachments
Optimize policy documents for faster mobile downloads
Reduce support manuals for customer sharing

faq.q: Does PDF compression affect readability?
faq.a: Compression can reduce detail at lower settings, so choose a balanced level.

faq.q: Can I adjust compression intensity?
faq.a: Yes. Use the range slider before generating the optimized output.

faq.q: Is this PDF compressor browser-only?
faq.a: Yes. The flow runs locally in the browser with no backend processing.

faq.q: Can I use this for business documents?
faq.a: Yes. Teams commonly compress reports, contracts, and presentations.

longTailPages.angle: Maximum size reduction for lightweight sharing

tools.title: Split PDF Online
tools.intent: Split one PDF into page-level files for reuse and archiving.
tools.description: Split PDF documents into single-page files with local processing.
useCases: Extract one signed page from a full contract
Separate textbook chapters for learning packs
Archive project appendices as standalone files

faq.q: Can I export all pages separately?
faq.a: Yes. Each page is exported as an individual PDF file.

faq.q: Will page order remain correct?
faq.a: Yes. Output follows the original PDF order.

faq.q: Is this split tool free?
faq.a: Yes. You can split PDFs directly in your browser.

faq.q: Do I need to install software?
faq.a: No. This is a browser-based tool.

tools.title: JPG to PDF Converter
tools.intent: Convert JPG and PNG images into one ordered PDF.
tools.description: Convert JPG and PNG images into one PDF with drag-and-drop ordering.
useCases: Convert expense receipts to one PDF report
Create PDF portfolios from design snapshots
Bundle classroom worksheets from photos

faq.q: Can I convert multiple JPG files together?
faq.a: Yes. Upload and reorder files to control final page sequence.

faq.q: Are PNG files supported?
faq.a: Yes. JPG and PNG files are both supported.

faq.q: Is image conversion secure?
faq.a: Yes. Files stay local in your browser session.

faq.q: Can I use this on iPhone or Android?
faq.a: Yes. Mobile browsers are supported.

tools.title: PDF to JPG Converter
tools.intent: Export PDF pages as JPG images for publishing and sharing.
tools.description: Convert PDF pages into JPG images for publishing and sharing.
useCases: Create website visuals from PDF documents
Extract presentation slides as images
Generate social-ready graphics from PDF pages

faq.q: Does each PDF page become a JPG file?
faq.a: Yes. Each page is exported as a separate JPG.

faq.q: Is output quality web-friendly?
faq.a: Yes. Export settings balance quality and file size.

faq.q: Can I convert large PDFs?
faq.a: Yes, though processing time depends on page count and device.

faq.q: Do files leave my device?
faq.a: No. Conversion is local in your browser.

tools.title: Protect PDF with Password
tools.intent: Add a password to your PDF in the browser—files stay on your device.
tools.description: Password-protect a PDF locally in your browser. AES-256 encryption with instant download—no upload to our servers.
useCases: Share confidential contracts with a password gate
Lock HR or payroll packets before email
Protect client deliverables for freelance handoffs

faq.q: Is my password sent to JoinMyPDF servers?
faq.a: No. Encryption runs entirely in your browser session. Your file and password never leave your device.

faq.q: What encryption standard is used?
faq.a: Protected PDFs use AES-256 encryption compatible with common PDF readers.

faq.q: Can I protect a PDF that already has a password?
faq.a: You need to unlock or re-export the file first if it is already encrypted.

faq.q: Will the protected PDF open in Adobe Acrobat and Preview?
faq.a: Yes. Standard PDF viewers prompt for the password you set here.

tools.title: Unlock PDF — Remove Password
tools.intent: Remove a PDF password in your browser—files stay on your device.
tools.description: Unlock a password-protected PDF locally in your browser. Decryption runs on your device with instant download—no upload to our servers.
useCases: Open client PDFs you are allowed to edit after entering the shared password
Remove protection before merging or compressing in JoinMyPDF
Save an unprotected copy for archival when policy allows

faq.q: Is my password sent to JoinMyPDF servers?
faq.a: No. Decryption runs entirely in your browser. Your file and password never leave your device.

faq.q: What if I enter the wrong password?
faq.a: You will see a clear message to try again. The file is not uploaded anywhere.

faq.q: Can I unlock PDFs I am not authorized to open?
faq.a: Only use this tool on files you own or are permitted to access. We do not bypass legal restrictions.

faq.q: Will the unlocked PDF work in Acrobat and Preview?
faq.a: Yes. The output is a standard unprotected PDF compatible with common viewers.
